candidates.why: Appears in the production/cost-of-revenue section above Gross profi as a separate same-level operating cost line, outside the already selected COGS rows ('Production costs' and 'Cost of sales of projects under development'). This suggests COGS-related D&A to add separately.

Generic depreciation line appears later in the operating section, but it is not clearly attributable specifically to the selected COGS bucket and may be a broader operating total. Excluding to avoid double counting.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation Share in result of associates and joint ventures
candidates.why: References associates/joint ventures rather than core operating COGS; should be ignored for this task.

variables.notes: Selected only the explicit production-section depreciation row that appears separate from the chosen COGS component rows. Excluded associate-related and generic depreciation rows to avoid non-operating contamination or double counting.

candidates.why: Explicit depreciation row in the SG&A area, shown alongside 'General and administration expenses' and before the broader operating lines. Since earlier mapping notes say depreciation-related rows were excluded from XSGA_COMPONENTS, this appears to be SG&A-related D&A that should be added separately.
Generic depreciation line in the later operating section may be a broader total spanning multiple buckets, not clearly specific to SG&A. Excluding conservatively to avoid double counting.

candidates.row_label: General administration and depreciation expenses - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balancing-value row appears to be a residual/constructed line rather than a clean standalone D&A row; not suitable when an explicit depreciation row exists.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation Share in result of associates and joint ventures
candidates.why: Associate/JV-related rather than SG&A operating overhead; ignore.

variables.notes: Used the explicit SG&A-area depreciation line only. Did not use generic 'Depreciation' rows because they are not clearly allocable to the selected XSGA bucket and may overlap with other operating buckets.
